## CSV References In Code

### `backend\base.py`
- Matched patterns: `\.csv\b, \bread_csv\b, \bcsv\.`
- Matching lines:
  - `225`: `Read ./strikeData/Nifty_strike_data.csv`
  - `231`: `f"{symbol}_strike_data.csv",`
  - `232`: `f"{symbol.lower()}_strike_data.csv",`
  - `233`: `f"{symbol.upper()}_strike_data.csv",`
  - `234`: `f"{symbol.capitalize()}_strike_data.csv",`
  - `235`: `f"{symbol.title()}_strike_data.csv",`

### `migrations\002_create_data_tables.sql`
- Matched patterns: `\.csv\b`
- Matching lines:
  - `6`: `--   1. cleaned_csvs/YYYY-MM-DD.csv  -> option_data (options & futures prices)`
  - `7`: `--   2. strikeData/*_strike_data.csv  -> spot_data (index closing prices)`
  - `8`: `--   3. expiryData/*.csv            -> expiry_calendar (weekly/monthly expiries)`
  - `9`: `--   4. Filter/base2.csv           -> trading_holidays (NSE trading halt periods)`
  - `10`: `--   5. Filter/STR*.csv            -> super_trend_segments (Super Trend periods)`
  - `86`: `-- Trading Holidays / Base2 Filter (replaces Filter/base2.csv)`
  - `98`: `-- Super Trend Segments (replaces Filter/STR*.csv)`

### `scripts\csv_schema_discovery.py`
- Matched patterns: `\.csv\b, \bcsv\.`
- Matching lines:
  - `51`: `re.compile(r"\.csv\b", re.IGNORECASE),`

  - `92`: `reader = csv.reader(f)`
